017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.springboot;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.function.*;
023
024import org.springframework.core.env.*;
025
026/**
027 * Bridges a Spring {@link Environment} into the Juneau {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.Settings Settings}
028 * singleton as a {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource PropertySource}.
029 *
030 * <p>
031 * Allows {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Value @Value("&#123;spring.property.key&#125;")} resolution
032 * against the same property sources that Spring Boot would resolve through
033 * {@code @org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value}: {@code application.yaml},
034 * {@code application.properties}, command-line args, environment variables, and any custom
035 * {@code org.springframework.core.env.PropertySource} that Spring loads.
036 *
037 * <h5 class='section'>Lifecycle:</h5>
038 * <p>
039 * Instances are auto-registered by {@link SpringBeanStore#SpringBeanStore(org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext,
040 * org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.BeanStore) SpringBeanStore}'s constructor and removed from
041 * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.Settings#removeSource(org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource)
042 * Settings} when {@link SpringBeanStore#clear()} is called.  Callers that want explicit lifecycle
043 * control can construct the source directly and add it via {@code Settings.get().addSource(...)}.
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Lazy resolution:</h5>
046 * <p>
047 * The {@code Environment} is resolved lazily on the first {@link #get(String)} call so that
048 * constructors that build the source from an {@code ApplicationContext} do not interact with the
049 * context until a lookup actually happens.  This matters for tests that mock the context and assert
050 * "no interactions" until a Spring-side bean is requested.
051 *
052 * <h5 class='section'>Ordering:</h5>
053 * <p>
054 * Because {@code Settings} walks property sources in reverse insertion order, the Spring
055 * {@code Environment} bridge — registered when Spring creates the bean store — shadows any
056 * classpath-default {@code juneau.cfg} loaded earlier through the SPI-discovered
057 * {@code ConfigPropertySourceProvider}.  Programmatic {@code addSource()} calls made after the
058 * Spring context is built (e.g. from a user-provided microservice {@code Config}) still win.
059 *
060 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
061 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ValueAnnotationBasics">{@code @Value} basics</a>
062 *    <li class='jc'>{@link SpringBeanStore}
063 * </ul>
064 */
065public class SpringEnvironmentPropertySource implements org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource {
066
067   private final Supplier<Environment> envSupplier;
068
069   // Guarded by the synchronized block in get(...); volatile reads cover the fast-path check.
070   private volatile boolean resolved;
071   @SuppressWarnings({
072      "java:S3077" // Publish-once reference: env is fully resolved before its single assignment (constructor or inside the synchronized block) and is never compound-mutated, so volatile visibility is sufficient.
073   })
074   private volatile Environment env;
075
076   /**
077    * Constructor that bridges a pre-built {@link Environment}.
078    *
079    * @param env The Spring environment to bridge.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
080    */
081   public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(Environment env) {
082      this.env = Objects.requireNonNull(env, "env");
083      this.envSupplier = null;
084      this.resolved = true;
085   }
086
087   /**
088    * Constructor that lazily resolves the {@link Environment} on first lookup.
089    *
090    * <p>
091    * Use this overload when wiring through {@link org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext}: pass
092    * {@code appContext::getEnvironment} so the application context isn't invoked until a lookup
093    * actually occurs.
094    *
095    * @param envSupplier Supplier of the Spring environment.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.  May return
096    *    <jk>null</jk>, in which case this source returns
097    *    {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult#missing()} for every key.
098    */
099   public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(Supplier<Environment> envSupplier) {
100      this.envSupplier = Objects.requireNonNull(envSupplier, "envSupplier");
101   }
102
103   @Override
104   @SuppressWarnings({
105      "java:S2589" // Double-checked locking: the inner `if (!resolved)` and its else-branch look redundant to Sonar's single-threaded symbolic execution but are essential for MT correctness (another thread can set resolved=true between the outer and inner checks).
106   })
107   public org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult get(String name) {
108      var e = env;
109      if (! resolved) {
110         synchronized (this) {
111            if (! resolved) {
112               // safeOpt swallows exceptions thrown by the supplier — e.g. a mocked
113               // ApplicationContext that returns null or throws during getEnvironment().
114               env = e = safeOpt(envSupplier::get).orElse(null);
115               resolved = true;
116            } else {
117               e = env;
118            }
119         }
120      }
121      if (e == null)
122         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
123      // Profile piggyback: a Juneau config-profile activation lookup resolves to Spring's active profiles, so a
124      // Spring-Boot deployment has a single source of truth for which profiles are active.  Only answered when the
125      // caller has NOT explicitly set juneau.profiles.active as a property (that exact key wins if present).
126      if (PROFILES_ACTIVE_KEY.equals(name) && ! e.containsProperty(name)) {
127         var active = e.getActiveProfiles();
128         if (active.length > 0)  // getActiveProfiles() never returns null per the Environment contract.
129            return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.present(opt(String.join(",", active)));
130         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
131      }
132      if (! e.containsProperty(name))
133         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
134      // Spring's getProperty() returns null only for unresolved placeholders, which
135      // containsProperty() already filtered out.  Wrap defensively anyway.
136      var v = e.getProperty(name);
137      return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.present(opt(v));
138   }
139
140   /** The Juneau config-profile activation key that piggybacks on Spring's active profiles. */
141   private static final String PROFILES_ACTIVE_KEY = "juneau.profiles.active";
142}
